label: Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state

node.label: Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state
node.definition: Checkpointing periodically persists weights, optimizer state, data position, random seeds, and metadata so interrupted or distributed runs can resume reproducibly.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state is to connect Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring, Object storage, sharding & checkpoints.
examples: substrate for Reproducibility, drift & continuous monitoring
substrate for Object storage, sharding & checkpoints
limitations: Results are sensitive to data quality, hyperparameters, compute budget, random variation, and evaluation contamination. Its direct dependencies include Distributed systems & networking, Databases & information retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state uses Distributed systems & networking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state uses Databases & information retrieval as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Checkpointing, recovery & experiment state uses Distributed, mixed-precision & memory-efficient training as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
